# W16T3 Implementation Plan

## Task Goal
Integrate SIMD and parallel execution into `dot()` while keeping the scalar baseline, error model, and panic contracts unchanged.

## Related Design Documents
- `design/12-matrix.md` §6.1
- `design/12-matrix.md` §6.1.1
- `design/12-matrix.md` §6.3
- `design/12-matrix.md` §10
- `design/08-simd.md` §6.5
- `design/09-parallel.md` §5.3
- `design/01-architecture.md` Decision 6

## Dependencies
- W16T2
- W13T3
- W14T4

## Files to Create/Modify
- `src/matrix/dot.rs`
- `tests/test_matrix.rs`

## Implementation Details

### Dispatch Skeleton
Use the documented path selection model:

```text
if a.ndim() != 1 -> InvalidArgument
if b.ndim() != 1 -> InvalidArgument
if a.len() != b.len() -> DimensionMismatch
match dispatch::select_exec_path(...) {
    ExecPath::Parallel => parallel::par_dot(as_ix1_view(a)?, as_ix1_view(b)?)
    ExecPath::Simd => simd::vector_dot(a, b)
    ExecPath::Serial => scalar::dot_impl(a, b)
}
```

### Integration Requirements
- `dispatch.rs` remains the only owner of serial-vs-parallel decisions.
- SIMD is only considered after the non-parallel path is chosen.
- SIMD path requires both inputs to be F-contiguous and aligned.
- `parallel::par_dot()` is called only after rank validation and `Ix1` narrowing.
- Nested parallelism must fall back to serial through the dispatch guard.

### Semantics
- All paths keep the same recoverable errors and panic behavior.
- Integer SIMD and parallel paths must remain exact, or not be used.
- Float and complex cross-path comparisons use the documented tolerance baseline.

## Test Plan
- `test_dot_simd_path_with_feature`
- `test_dot_parallel_path`
- `test_dot_large_vector_parallel_threshold`
- `test_dot_nested_parallel_falls_back`
- `test_dot_simd_parallel_combined_consistency`
- `test_dot_parallel_threshold_boundary`
- `test_dot_float_tolerance_across_paths`

## Acceptance Criteria
- `dot()` consults `dispatch.rs` instead of duplicating threshold logic.
- SIMD path is used only when both contiguity and alignment preconditions are met.
- Parallel path reuses `parallel::par_dot()` after `Ix1` narrowing.
- Nested parallel calls do not create a second parallel region.
- Results remain consistent with the scalar baseline.

## Development Workflow
1. Add path-selection and fallback tests.
2. Integrate `select_exec_path()` into `dot_impl()`.
3. Add SIMD bridge call.
4. Add parallel bridge call with `as_ix1_view()`.
5. Re-run matrix tests under default, `simd`, `parallel`, and combined features.
